rulings.Case Title: T&C PROPERTIES, LLC VS. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Renewed Motion And Renewed Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs (Code Civ. Proc. Section 1008(B))
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 19, 2024 line 1. RESPONDENT ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC NOTICE OF RENEWED MOTION AND RENEWED MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS (CODE CIV. PROC. SECTION 1008(B)) Hearing Required. Counsel to appear in-person. On August 19, 2024 the Court requested delivery of courtesy copies of the parties' respective filings in support of and in opposition to this motion, having only received a copy of the reply papers at that time. The purported "courtesy" copy of the moving papers delivered to the Court is a 3-inch stack of unfastened, unstapled and untabbed papers. The papers are held together by two rubber bands. It is impossible to review the papers without the removal of the rubber bands. Upon the removal of the rubber band nothing holds these loose papers together. Opposing party delivered a "courtesy" copy of the Notice of Errata consisting of 1.5-inch stack of unfastened, unstapled and untabbed papers. The papers are held together by a rubber band. It is impossible to review the papers without the removal of the rubber band. Upon the removal of the rubber band nothing holds these loose papers together. The "courtesy" copies will be available for counsel to pick up at the roll-call of the law and motion calendar to be fastened, tabbed and stapled and returned to the Court. Parties to meet and confer on the new date for the continued hearing after the Court receives usable courtesy copies. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANTONIO CASTELLUCCI VS. KAREN MINNICH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 19, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT KAREN MINNICH DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on September 19, 2024 Plaintiff to (1) file a declaration of David Lagomarsino describing in detail the circumstances surrounding the execution of the POS attached as Exhibit B to the First Amended Complaint, including specifically, the date at time the words "San Francisco, CA 94109" where included on the POS and the date and time this POS was delivered to counsel; if declarant is in possession of copies of the mailed envelope or pictures of the envelope, such copies may be attached to the declaration; (2) file a declaration of David Foran describing the circumstances under which the POS described above was received by counsel for filing. The originals of the POS filed as Exhibit B to Complaint and Exhibit B to the Amended Complaint to be presented to the Court at the hearing. David Lagomarsino and David Foran to be present in-person at the hearing. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES, LP, A CALIFORNIA VS. LAWAYNE LONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 19, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT LAWAYNE LONG, DARRELL PHILLIPS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to October 18, 2024 for supplemental briefing. No later than October 7, 2024 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief addressing the following: (1) authority in support of the statement in the opposition that "also not a novel concept is the possibility that a tenancy can be subject to both state and local law." Plaintiff shall discuss CC 1946.2 (i)(1)(A) in conjunction with the authorities that Plaintiff cites for the above proposition; (2) authority and facts appearing on the face of the complaint (specify paragraphs and page numbers) supporting Plaintiff's assertion in opposition that "[tenancy is] exempt from both [TPA 2019 and SFRO] for purposes of the eviction."; (3) address whether Notice complies with SFRO 37.9(c) ("unless the landlord informs the tenant in writing on or before the date upon which notice to vacate is given of the grounds under which possession is sought") as to Darrell Phillips given that due to the placement of the definition of the terms "you" and "yours" appearing right before the paragraph discussing just cause for eviction terminating "your" i.e. "Lawayne Long's" tenancy ONLY pursuant to "San Francisco Ordinance 18-22, Sec. 37.9 EVICTIONS." Defendant may file a reply to the supplemental briefing no later than October 14, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
